raceName: Cadel Evans Great Ocean Road Race
tldr: Tobias Lund Andresen out-sprinted Matthew Brennan for the win on the Geelong circuit, with Australian rider Brady Gilmore third. Brennan's second on home Aussie soil was an early-season validation of Visma's young Classics prospect well before his Arenberg lead-out for Van Aert two months later.

narrative.headline: Lund Andresen wins, Brennan second on home Aussie soil
narrative.openingMoves: Geelong circuit, the traditional WorldTour day after Tour Down Under finishes. A reduced-bunch sprint result was always the most likely scenario; the climbing inland off the coast is enough to thin the pure sprinters but not enough to gap the puncheur-sprinters.
narrative.raceUnfolds: Standard Geelong development — early break, controlled chase, reduced bunch into the finishing circuit.
narrative.decision: Came down to a sprint that the Decathlon-Brennan-Gilmore order resolved cleanly.
narrative.finale: Lund Andresen's spring 2026 starts to take shape here; he'd repeat on the Gent-Wevelgem podium in March. Brennan's second was the first early-season pointer that Visma's developmental plan around him was on schedule.

teamStories.narrative: Decathlon's signing of Lund Andresen had been one of the quieter off-season moves; his early-season pace makes it look louder in retrospect.

teamStories.verdict: Brennan's first big result of the year
teamStories.narrative: Brennan, billed as Visma's emerging Classics leader, took second on home Aussie soil — a clean early-season validation that ran through to his Arenberg lead-out for Van Aert at Roubaix.

riderPerformances.name: Matthew Brennan
riderPerformances.narrative: Second on the Geelong circuit, behind Lund Andresen in a reduced-bunch sprint. The first major early-season pointer for the Visma development plan that would land him as Van Aert's Arenberg lead-out two months later at Roubaix.

aftermath.headline: Australian summer closes
aftermath.body: Cadel Evans Race is always the final WorldTour day of the Aussie summer. Lund Andresen and Brennan leaving Geelong as 1-2 was a useful weighting of two of the season's emerging finishers.

sources.title: Cadel Evans Great Ocean Road Race 2026 result
